content_en:
' A tax levied on disbelivers under Muslim rule in piace of zakah.

Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah went with the delegation to Yemen and according to the instructions received from the Prophet 38, fulfilled his duty. The laudatory sentences, the phrases of praise expressed by the Prophet 38 became his life-savings for Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . This was a unique honour for him, not shared by any of the other Companions.

In the wars of Badr, Uhud, Khandaq, Banu Quraidhah, and in the battles of Salasai, Dimashq, Fahal, Hamas and Yarmook and in many other less important battles, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 proved his courage and daring. He was present at the signing of the Treaty of Hudaibiyah; in fact he was one of the signatories to it. He also accompanied the Prophet 38 on Hajjaht-ul-Wida’a (the last pilgrimage).

Seeing this all the Muhdjireen and Ansar also crowded around him and swore allegiance to him; all differences were resolved, and everyone breathed a sigh of relief. Thus, in a very simple yet well-planned manner he resolved a_ situation which was of critical significance for the Ummah. Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 was known to be a very successful General, but he was also known as a perceptive diplomat

' Original inhabitants of Al-Madinah. 2 The migrants from Makkah.

and skilful negotiator who could find peaceful solutions for potentially violent and problematic situations. This was the reason why advantage was taken of this dexterity and skill of his in difficult situations to the very end of his life. The Prophet 38 gave him the title of Amtin-ul-Ummah (Trustee of his Ummah), Abu Bakr Siddiq # offered him the first Caliphate and ‘Umar Farooq % said he would have made him Caliph if he had been alive.

After conquering the famous key city of Hamas in Syria General Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah , leading the Muslim army, moved on. He appointed ‘Ubadah bin Samit 4% to look after Hamas. All the settlements on the way were conquered. Lazaqiah was a fortified town, this was besieged but it did not look like it would be easy to conquer it. Here the master strategist, General Abu ‘Ubaidah bin AlJarrah -% planned a different modus operandi which amazed both friends and enemies. He ordered the Mujahideen to dig trenches in the open fields near the city. Once this was done he ordered his troops to end the siege. When the siege was lifted and his army withdrew, the people of the city were overjoyed. They thought all their problems had been solved. When the army withdrew and was no longer visible the people came out of the city. The whole day there was the usual hustle and bustle in the town, doors were left open and people kept moving in and out of the town. As soon as the sun set, the doors of the city were closed. He then asked his men to hide in the trenches they had dug, taking advantage of the darkness of the night. When morning came the doors of the city were opened and the people of the city went about as per their routine, moving in and out of the city. Suddenly the Mujahideen came out of the trenches and entered the city and very easily conquered it. On the battlefront digging of trenches is today an accepted part of military strategy. It was Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 who first introduced these tactics in a battle.

Yarmook is the name of a river, which meets the river Jordan and about thirty miles further up the river Yarmook makes a circle and flows on. From here it flows into a huge plain. The famous war that was fought here during the Caliphate of Abu Bakr Siddiq came to

be known as the war of Yarmook. It was the Roman army, consisting of two hundred thousand men, which fought here against the much smaller force of the Mujahideen consisting of forty thousand men only. When Khalid bin Waleed reached here after a very long and tedious journey from Iraq, he was worried to see the scattered forces of the Muslims under several different leaders. Among them were different troops under Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah #%, Yazid bin Abi Sufyan, Sharjeel bin Masana and the fourth one under ‘Amr bin ‘Aas SI +, Khalid bin Waleed # gathered everyone together and addressed them sorrowfully. He said that the enemy had entered the field very well-prepared and also outnumbered them by several times. If they opposed him, divided into so many groups, they had no hopes of winning. In his opinion they should agree to hand over the command each day to a different leader; thus the Muslim army would present a picture of unity, oneness and understanding, and move rapidly towards victory. The command on the first day could be handed over to him, and one by one the other four could take over on the following days. The whole army was relieved and happy to hear this. After having been given command on the first day Khalid bin Waleed - trained the Mujihideen in the light of his experiences. The two armies faced each other and there was very heavy fighting. The Romans lost more than a hundred thousand men on this very first day. The clanging of swords and the shouts and screams of the men could be heard from all sides. The Mujahideen were busy trying to wipe out the Romans, and advancing under the leadership of Khalid bin Waleed #, when a messenger arrived from the capital, AlMadinah with a hand written message which he gave to Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . He put it in his pocket and did not tell anyone about it. When the Muslims had won convincingly and Khalid bin Waleed .4, was standing in the field as the victorious General of a victorious army, he handed over the message very respectfully to him. Khalid bin Waleed 4%, was deeply moved as it carried the news of the passing away of Abu Bakr Siddiq . He said:

“To Allah we belong and to Him we return.”

Further he was informed that ‘Umar Farooq :# was the new Caliph, and thanked Allah Almighty for this. After this he read the shocking

news that he had been removed from his command and Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 had been appointed Commander of the Muslim army. He immediately handed over command and expressed his willing desire to fight under him as an ordinary soldier and said:

“Abu ‘Ubaidah, may Allah shower His Blessings on you; why did you not tell me as soon as you received the letter?”

The Amin-ul-Ummah, Abu ‘Ubaidah replied:

“He did not like to disturb him in the battlefield and stop his advancing steps. We do not want the power of this world, neither do we work for this world. We are all brothers in working to please Allah Almighty.”

Displaying exemplary patience and fortitude in this situation, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 2 proved that the training and nurturing of the Prophet 38 had left very deep-rooted and pleasant effects on him. He was not affected or elated by having been given command of the army. His heart had only one desire - the glorification of Islam. And Khalid bin Waleed #% too did not have any regrets that the command was taken away from him. Certainly this again was due to the education and training imparted by the Prophet 3 that a race, which had been the most uncivilised was affected and influenced so deeply by him.

After the conquest of Damascus Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah informed the Caliph ‘Umar Farooq - that the people of Damascus were very eager to see you and wanted to present you the keys of the city. As soon as he heard this ‘Umar Farooq -% set out for Syria. He asked the people waiting to receive him about the whereabouts of his brother. The people were surprised and asked him, who brother was. He answered that he was asking about Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 2. Then they pointed out that he was just approaching. ‘Umar Farooq went forward and embraced him warmly and then accompanied him to his house. When he saw the very austere lifestyle of Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah he was very surprised. There were no worldly goods in the house, nothing but his weapons - a sword, bow and arrows, and _his saddle. ‘Umar Farooq was amazed that a man of such high status had chosen to live such a simple life. He said:

“Abu ‘Ubaidah, it is very surprising that even though you have been appointed to such a high office, you have not made anything for yourself.”

Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah .% answered that what he had was sufficient for him. ‘Umar Farooq answered that this was exactly what he had expected from him, that he would remain true and constant to the norms of trustworthiness.

Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah remained loyal to ‘Umar Farooq during his entire Caliphate and co-operated with him. Except in one instance he never disobeyed him. Can you guess what was this one instance when he disobeyed him?

Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah # was performing his duties of commanding the Muslim forces in Syria and conquering vast territories very rapidly; the whole of Syria had been conquered and Islamic empire had spread far and wide and graph of victories was at its peak. What transpired was that at this time the fatal disease of plague spread in the city, as it never had before. People were effected prey to it very rapidly. When ’Umar Farooq -% came to know of this he sent a messenger witha letter for Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah #s. He wrote in the letter that he had some important business with him, and wanted him to make preparations and immediately set out for AlMadinah to meet him. He was not to delay; if the letter reached him at night, he should set out before morning; if it reached him in the morning, he should set out before night fell.
content_id:
Pajak yang dikenakan kepada orang-orang kafir di bawah pemerintahan Muslim sebagai pengganti zakah.

Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah pergi bersama delegasi ke Yaman dan sesuai dengan instruksi yang diterima dari Nabi ﷺ, ia menunaikan tugasnya. Kalimat-kalimat pujian yang diungkapkan oleh Nabi ﷺ menjadi bekal hidup bagi Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah. Ini merupakan kehormatan istimewa baginya yang tidak dimiliki oleh sahabat lainnya.

Dalam perang Badr, Uhud, Khandaq, Banu Quraidhah, dan dalam pertempuran Salasai, Dimasyq, Fahal, Hamas dan Yarmuk serta dalam banyak pertempuran lain yang lebih kecil, Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah membuktikan keberanian dan kegigihannya. Ia hadir pada penandatanganan Perjanjian Hudaibiyah; bahkan ia adalah salah satu penandatangannya. Ia juga menemani Nabi ﷺ dalam Hajjat-ul-Wida' (haji terakhir).

Melihat ini semua kaum Muhajirin dan Anshar juga berkerumun di sekelilingnya dan berbaiat kepadanya; semua perbedaan terselesaikan, dan semua orang bernapas lega. Dengan demikian, secara sederhana namun terencana dengan baik, ia menyelesaikan situasi yang sangat kritis bagi ummah. Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah dikenal sebagai Jenderal yang sangat berhasil, tetapi ia juga dikenal sebagai diplomat yang tajam

Penduduk asli Al-Madinah. 2 Para pendatang dari Makkah.

dan negosiator ulung yang mampu menemukan solusi damai untuk situasi-situasi yang berpotensi penuh kekerasan dan problematik. Inilah alasan mengapa kecakapan dan keahliannya dimanfaatkan dalam situasi-situasi sulit hingga akhir hayatnya. Nabi ﷺ memberinya gelar Amin-ul-Ummah (Orang Kepercayaan Ummah-nya), Abu Bakr Siddiq menawarkan kepadanya kekhalifahan pertama, dan 'Umar Farooq berkata bahwa ia akan menjadikannya khalifah seandainya ia masih hidup.

Setelah menaklukkan kota kunci terkenal Hamas di Suriah, Jenderal Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah, yang memimpin pasukan Muslim, bergerak maju. Ia menunjuk 'Ubadah bin Samit untuk menjaga Hamas. Semua pemukiman di sepanjang jalan ditaklukkan. Lazaqiah adalah kota yang dibentengi, kota ini dikepung tetapi tampaknya tidak mudah untuk menaklukkannya. Di sinilah sang ahli strategi ulung, Jenderal Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah merencanakan modus operandi yang berbeda yang mengejutkan kawan maupun lawan. Ia memerintahkan para mujahidin untuk menggali parit di ladang terbuka dekat kota. Setelah selesai, ia memerintahkan pasukannya untuk mengakhiri pengepungan. Ketika pengepungan dicabut dan pasukannya mundur, penduduk kota sangat gembira. Mereka mengira semua masalah mereka telah terselesaikan. Ketika pasukan mundur dan tidak lagi terlihat, penduduk keluar dari kota. Sepanjang hari ada kesibukan biasa di kota, pintu-pintu dibiarkan terbuka dan orang-orang terus keluar masuk kota. Begitu matahari terbenam, pintu-pintu kota ditutup. Ia kemudian meminta anak buahnya bersembunyi di parit yang telah mereka gali, memanfaatkan kegelapan malam. Ketika pagi tiba, pintu-pintu kota dibuka dan penduduk kota menjalani rutinitas mereka seperti biasa, keluar masuk kota. Tiba-tiba para mujahidin keluar dari parit dan memasuki kota serta dengan mudah menaklukkannya. Di medan perang, penggalian parit saat ini merupakan bagian yang diterima dari strategi militer. Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah-lah yang pertama kali memperkenalkan taktik ini dalam pertempuran.

Yarmuk adalah nama sebuah sungai yang bertemu dengan Sungai Yordan, dan sekitar tiga puluh mil ke hulu, Sungai Yarmuk membentuk lingkaran dan mengalir terus. Dari sini ia mengalir ke dataran luas. Perang terkenal yang terjadi di sini pada masa kekhalifahan Abu Bakr Siddiq kemudian

dikenal sebagai Perang Yarmuk. Pasukan Romawi yang terdiri dari dua ratus ribu orang bertempur di sini melawan pasukan mujahidin yang jauh lebih kecil yang hanya terdiri dari empat puluh ribu orang. Ketika Khalid bin Walid tiba di sini setelah perjalanan yang sangat panjang dan melelahkan dari Irak, ia khawatir melihat pasukan Muslim yang terpencar di bawah beberapa pemimpin yang berbeda. Di antara mereka terdapat pasukan-pasukan berbeda di bawah Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah, Yazid bin Abi Sufyan, Sharjeel bin Masana, dan yang keempat di bawah 'Amr bin 'Aas. Khalid bin Walid mengumpulkan semua orang dan berbicara kepada mereka dengan penuh keprihatinan. Ia berkata bahwa musuh telah memasuki medan perang dengan persiapan yang sangat matang dan juga melebihi jumlah mereka berkali-kali lipat. Jika mereka menghadapinya dalam keadaan terpecah-pecah menjadi begitu banyak kelompok, mereka tidak memiliki harapan untuk menang. Menurutnya, mereka harus setuju untuk menyerahkan komando setiap hari kepada pemimpin yang berbeda; dengan demikian pasukan Muslim akan menampilkan gambaran persatuan, keesaan dan saling pengertian, serta bergerak cepat menuju kemenangan. Komando pada hari pertama dapat diserahkan kepadanya, dan satu per satu empat pemimpin lainnya dapat mengambil alih pada hari-hari berikutnya. Seluruh pasukan merasa lega dan senang mendengar ini. Setelah diberi komando pada hari pertama, Khalid bin Walid melatih para mujahidin berdasarkan pengalamannya. Kedua pasukan saling berhadapan dan terjadilah pertempuran yang sangat sengit. Pasukan Romawi kehilangan lebih dari seratus ribu orang pada hari pertama ini saja. Dentingan pedang serta teriakan dan jeritan para prajurit terdengar dari segala penjuru. Para mujahidin sibuk berusaha menghancurkan pasukan Romawi dan maju di bawah kepemimpinan Khalid bin Walid, ketika seorang utusan tiba dari ibu kota, Al-Madinah, dengan pesan tertulis yang ia serahkan kepada Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah. Ia memasukkannya ke dalam sakunya dan tidak memberitahu siapa pun tentang itu. Ketika kaum Muslimin telah menang secara meyakinkan dan Khalid bin Walid berdiri di medan perang sebagai Jenderal pemenang dari pasukan yang menang, ia menyerahkan pesan itu dengan penuh hormat kepadanya. Khalid bin Walid sangat terharu karena pesan itu membawa berita wafatnya Abu Bakr Siddiq. Ia berkata:

"Sesungguhnya kita milik Allah dan kepada-Nya kita kembali."

Selanjutnya ia diberitahu bahwa 'Umar Farooq adalah khalifah baru, dan ia bersyukur kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa atas hal ini. Setelah itu ia membaca berita yang mengejutkan

bahwa ia telah dicopot dari komandonya dan Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah telah ditunjuk sebagai Panglima pasukan Muslim. Ia segera menyerahkan komando dan menyatakan keinginannya dengan tulus untuk bertempur di bawahnya sebagai prajurit biasa dan berkata:

"Abu 'Ubaidah, semoga Allah melimpahkan rahmat-Nya kepadamu; mengapa engkau tidak memberitahuku segera setelah engkau menerima surat itu?"

Amin-ul-Ummah, Abu 'Ubaidah menjawab:

"Ia tidak ingin mengganggunya di medan perang dan menghentikan langkah majunya. Kita tidak menginginkan kekuasaan dunia ini, dan kita tidak pula bekerja untuk dunia ini. Kita semua bersaudara dalam bekerja untuk meraih ridha Allah Yang Maha Kuasa."

Dengan menunjukkan kesabaran dan ketabahan yang luar biasa dalam situasi ini, Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah membuktikan bahwa didikan dan bimbingan Nabi ﷺ telah meninggalkan pengaruh yang sangat mendalam dan indah pada dirinya. Ia tidak terpengaruh atau bangga karena diberi komando atas pasukan. Hatinya hanya memiliki satu keinginan yaitu kemuliaan Islam. Dan Khalid bin Walid pun tidak memiliki penyesalan apa pun bahwa komando diambil darinya. Tentu saja ini kembali karena pendidikan dan pelatihan yang diberikan oleh Nabi ﷺ sehingga suatu bangsa yang dulunya paling tidak beradab terpengaruh dan tersentuh begitu mendalam olehnya.

Setelah penaklukan Damaskus, Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah memberitahu Khalifah 'Umar Farooq bahwa penduduk Damaskus sangat ingin bertemu dengannya dan ingin mempersembahkan kunci kota kepadanya. Begitu mendengar ini, 'Umar Farooq berangkat ke Suriah. Ia bertanya kepada orang-orang yang menunggu untuk menyambutnya tentang keberadaan saudaranya. Orang-orang terkejut dan bertanya kepadanya siapa saudaranya. Ia menjawab bahwa ia bertanya tentang Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah. Kemudian mereka menunjukkan bahwa ia baru saja mendekat. 'Umar Farooq maju dan memeluknya dengan hangat lalu menemaninya ke rumahnya. Ketika ia melihat gaya hidup Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah yang sangat sederhana, ia sangat terkejut. Tidak ada barang-barang duniawi di rumah itu, tidak ada apa pun selain senjatanya yaitu pedang, busur dan anak panah, serta pelananya. 'Umar Farooq takjub bahwa seorang yang berstatus begitu tinggi memilih untuk hidup sesederhana itu. Ia berkata:

"Abu 'Ubaidah, sungguh mengherankan bahwa meskipun engkau telah ditunjuk pada jabatan yang begitu tinggi, engkau tidak menyiapkan apa pun untuk dirimu sendiri."

Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah menjawab bahwa apa yang ia miliki sudah cukup baginya. 'Umar Farooq menjawab bahwa inilah tepat yang ia harapkan darinya, bahwa ia akan tetap setia dan konsisten pada norma-norma amanah.

Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah tetap setia kepada 'Umar Farooq selama masa kekhalifahannya dan bekerja sama dengannya. Kecuali dalam satu hal, beliau tidak pernah menentangnya. Dapatkah Anda menebak apa satu hal yang membuat beliau menentangnya?

Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah # sedang menjalankan tugasnya memimpin pasukan Muslim di Syam dan menaklukkan wilayah-wilayah luas dengan sangat cepat; seluruh Syam telah ditaklukkan dan kekuasaan Islam telah menyebar luas, serta grafik kemenangan berada di puncaknya. Yang terjadi adalah pada saat itu penyakit wabah yang mematikan menyebar di kota tersebut, seperti yang belum pernah terjadi sebelumnya. Orang-orang terjangkit dengan sangat cepat. Ketika 'Umar Farooq -% mengetahui hal ini, beliau mengirim utusan dengan surat untuk Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah #s. Beliau menulis dalam surat itu bahwa ada urusan penting dengannya, dan menginginkan agar beliau bersiap-siap dan segera berangkat ke Al-Madinah untuk menemuinya. Beliau tidak boleh menunda; jika surat itu sampai kepadanya di malam hari, beliau harus berangkat sebelum pagi; jika sampai di pagi hari, beliau harus berangkat sebelum malam tiba.

The laudatory sentences, the phrases of praise expressed by النبي ﷺ 38 became his الحياة-savings for Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . This was a unique honour for him, not shared by any of the other الصحابة. In the wars of Badr, Uhud, Khandaq, Banu Quraidhah, and in the المعركةs of Salasai, Dimashq, Fahal, Hamas and Yarmook and in many other less المهم المعركةs, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 proved his الشجاعة and daring. He ordered the المجاهدين to dig trenches in the open fields near the city. حين this was done he ordered his القوات to end the siege. عندما the siege was lifted and his الجيش withdrew, the people of the city were overjoyed. They thought all their problems had been solved. عندما the الجيش withdrew and was no longer visible the people came out of the city. The whole day there was the usual hustle and bustle in the town, doors were left open and people kept moving in and out of the town. As soon as the sun set, the doors of the city were closed. Yarmook is the name of a river, which meets the river Jordan and about thirty miles further up the river Yarmook makes a circle and flows on. From here it flows into a huge plain. The famous war that was قاتل here خلال the الخلافة of أبو بكر Siddiq came to be known as the war of Yarmook. It was the Roman الجيش, consisting of two hundred thousand men, which قاتل here against the much smaller force of the المجاهدين consisting of forty thousand men only. عندما Khalid bin Waleed reached here بعد a very long and tedious journey from Iraq, he was worried to see the scattered forces of the المسلمون under several different leaders. Among them were different القوات under Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah #%, Yazid bin Abi Sufyan, Sharjeel bin Masana and the رابعاً one under ‘Amr bin ‘Aas SI +, Khalid bin Waleed # gathered everyone together and addressed them sorrowfully. قال that the العدو had entered the field very well-prepared and أيضاً outnumbered them by several times. إذا they opposed him, divided into لذا many groups, they had no hopes of winning. In his opinion they should agree to hand over the command each day to a different leader; وهكذا the مسلم الجيش would present a picture of unity, oneness and الفهم, and move rapidly towards النصر. The command on the أولاً day could be handed over to him, and one by one the other four could take over on the following days. The whole الجيش was relieved and happy to hear this. بعد having been given command on the أولاً day Khalid bin Waleed - trained the Mujihideen in the light of his experiences. The two armies faced بعضهم بعضاً and there was very heavy fighting. The Romans lost more than a hundred thousand men on this very أولاً day. The clanging of السيفs and the shouts and screams of the men could be heard from all sides. The المجاهدين were busy trying to wipe out the Romans, and advancing under the leadership of Khalid bin Waleed #, عندما a messenger arrived from the capital, AlMadinah with a hand written message which he gave to Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah . He put it in his pocket and did not tell anyone about it. عندما the المسلمون had won convincingly and Khalid bin Waleed .4, was standing in the field as the victorious General of a victorious الجيش, he handed over the message very respectfully to him. Khalid bin Waleed 4%, was deeply moved as it carried the news of the passing away of أبو بكر Siddiq . قال: “To الله we belong and to Him we return.” Further he was informed that ‘عمر Farooq :# was the new Caliph, and thanked الله Almighty for this. بعد this he read the shocking news that he had been removed from his command and Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4 had been appointed القائد of the مسلم الجيش. He immediately handed over command and expressed his willing desire to fight under him as an ordinary soldier and said: “Abu ‘Ubaidah, may الله shower His البركات on you; why did you not tell me as soon as you received the letter?” The آمين-ul-الأمة, Abu ‘Ubaidah replied: “He did not like to disturb him in the المعركةfield and stop his advancing steps. We do not want the power of this الدنيا, neither do we work for this الدنيا. We are all brothers in working to please الله Almighty.” Displaying exemplary الصبر and fortitude in this situation, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 2 proved that the training and nurturing of النبي ﷺ 38 had left very deep-rooted and pleasant effects on him. He was not affected or elated by having been given command of the الجيش. His القلب had only one desire - the glorification of الإسلام. And Khalid bin Waleed #% أيضاً did not have any regrets that the command was taken away from him. Certainly this again was بسبب the education and training imparted by النبي ﷺ 3 that a race, which had been the most uncivilised was affected and influenced لذا deeply by him. بعد the الفتح of Damascus Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah informed the Caliph ‘عمر Farooq - that the people of Damascus were very eager to see you and wanted to present you the keys of the city. As soon as he heard this ‘عمر Farooq -% set out for Syria. He asked the people waiting to receive him about the whereabouts of his الأخ. The people were surprised and asked him, who الأخ was. رد that he was asking about Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 2. Then they pointed out that he was just approaching. ‘عمر Farooq went forward and embraced him warmly and then accompanied him to his house. عندما he saw the very austere lifestyle of Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah he was very surprised. There were no worldly goods in the house, nothing but his weapons - a السيف, bow and arrows, and _his saddle. ‘عمر Farooq was amazed that a man of such high status had chosen to live such a simple الحياة. قال: “Abu ‘Ubaidah, it is very surprising that حتى وإن you have been appointed to such a high office, you have not made anything for yourself.” Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah .% answered that what he had was sufficient for him. ‘عمر Farooq answered that this was exactly what he had expected from him, that he would remain true and constant to the norms of trustworthiness. Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah remained loyal to ‘عمر Farooq خلال his entire الخلافة and co-operated with him. Except in one instance he never disobeyed him. Can you guess what was this one instance عندما he disobeyed him? Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah # was performing his duties of commanding the مسلم forces in Syria and conquering vast territories very rapidly; the whole of Syria had been conquered and Islamic empire had spread far and wide and graph of victories was at its peak. What transpired was that at this time the fatal المرض of plague spread in the city, as it never had قبل. People were effected prey to it very rapidly. عندما ’عمر Farooq -% came لمعرفة of this he sent a messenger witha letter for Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah #s. كتب in the letter that he had some المهم business with him, and wanted him to make preparations and immediately set out for AlMadinah to meet him. He was not to delay; إذا the letter reached him at night, he should set out قبل morning; إذا it reached him in the morning, he should set out قبل night fell.
